title: Invalid JSON serialization when NaN and Infinity values are present
description: ### What happens? `NaN` and `Infinity` are not valid values according to the JSON spec. It's possible to produce invalid JSON in DuckDB by serializing data that contains `NaN` and `Infinity` to JSON. For example, `json_object('foo', 0/0)` produces `{"foo":NaN}`. I'd expect this to be `{"foo":null}` instead. For reference, here's the behavior in JS: ### To Reproduce ``` SELECT json_object('foo', 0/0); ``` ### OS: MacOS 15.3.1 arm64 ### DuckDB Version: 1.2.2 ### DuckDB Client: Shell ### Hardware: _No response_ ### Full Name: Tong Liu ### Affiliation: Watershed ### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build. I have tested with a stable release ### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue? Yes ### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have ### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue? - [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value FLOAT, description TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, description) VALUES (1, NULLIF(0/0, 0/0), 'NaN value');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, description) VALUES (2, NULLIF(1/0, 1/0), 'Infinity value');
SELECT CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'null' ELSE value::TEXT END AS value FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'null' ELSE value::TEXT END AS value FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
key_bug_features: Invalid JSON serialization for NaN and Infinity values
